ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE SET FOR AUG-24-2021 CONTINUED TO NOV-23-2021 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610 FOR FAILURE TO FILE PROOF OF SERVICE ON DEFENDANT(S) AND OBTAIN ANSWER(S), OR ENTER DEFAULT(S). NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE APR-01-2021 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF OBINNA UGORJI'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO SERVE DEPOSITION SUBPOENAS PRIOR TO SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO SERVE DEPOSITION SUBPOENAS PRIOR TO SERVICE OF COMPLAINT: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF IS ABLE TO SERVE SUBPOENA ON UBER FOR PRODUCTION OF RECORDS IDENTIFYING THE PASSENGER(S) WHO PLACED THE UBER RIDESHARE REQUEST AND/OR RODE WITH PLAINTIFF ON JUNE 9, 2018 WHEN PLAINTIFF WAS ASSAULTED AND TO THE EXTENT NECESSARY ISSUE A DEPOSITION SUBPOENA FOR PERSONAL APPEARS OF THAT PASSENGER FOR THE SOLE PURPOSE OF OBTAINING THE NAME AND CONTACT INFORMATION OF THE PASSENGER WHO ALLEGEDLY ASSAULTED AND BATTERED PLAINTIFF. COURT TO SIGN THE PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED BY THE PREVAILING PARTY. JUDGE PRO TEM: ROBERT KANE; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)
ORDER GRANTING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO SERVE DEPOSITION SUBPOENAS PRIOR TO SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Serve Deposition Subpoenas Prior To Service Of Summons And Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-01-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF OBINNA UGORJI's Motion For Leave To Serve Deposition Subpoenas Prior To Service Of Summons And Complaint.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's Motion to Serve Deposition Subpoenas Prior to Service of Complaint: Plaintiff's motion is granted. Plaintiff is able to serve subpoena on Uber for production of records identifying the passenger(s) who placed the Uber rideshare request and/or rode with Plaintiff on June 9, 2018 when Plaintiff was assaulted and to the extent necessary issue a deposition subpoena for personal appears of that passenger for the sole purpose of obtaining the name and contact information of the passenger who allegedly assaulted and battered Plaintiff.

Court to sign the proposed order submitted by the prevailing party.

Judge Pro Tem: Robert Kane; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
